title: PS-69087 Phase 6: org provisioning FileShare test coverage
body:
## Summary

Closes Phase 6 FileShare coverage gaps from PS-69087.

| # | Method | Test class |
|---|--------|------------|
| 63 | `IgnitionSystem.GetFileShares` | `IgnitionSystemTests` |
| 10 | `IgnitionSystem.CopyCustomReportFiles` | `IgnitionSystemTests` (org-from-template) |
| 60 | `ProjectSystem.CopyPhysicalFiles` | `ProjectCopyFilesTests` |
| 48 | `StaticSystem.CreateStaticFileID` | `StaticFileIDTests` |

## Changes

- Extended `Records/Portfolios/IgnitionSystemTests.cs` with GetFileShares and custom-report copy assertions
- Added `Records/Projects/ProjectCopyFilesTests.cs` and `Records/StaticSystem/StaticFileIDTests.cs`
- Updated `coverage-progress.md` and `PS-69087-fileshare-methods.playlist`

## Test plan

Focused filter: IgnitionSystemTests (GetFileShares + CopyCustomReportFiles), ProjectCopyFilesTests, StaticFileIDTests
author: AdamCollins

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR targets PS-69087 Phase 6 by filling remaining FileShare-related test coverage gaps around org provisioning helpers, and updates the supporting tracking artifacts used to manage the epic’s coverage checklist.

**Changes:**
- Added/extended ParallelIntegrationTests to cover `IgnitionSystem.GetFileShares`, `IgnitionSystem.CopyCustomReportFiles`, `ProjectSystem.CopyPhysicalFiles`, and `StaticSystem.CreateStaticFileID`.
- Added Phase 6 entries to the PS-69087 playlist and introduced a Phase checklist tracking document for the non-TC-files branch work.
- Updated test assertions to validate concrete filesystem side effects (file existence/size/pathing) for the newly covered methods.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| docs/temp/tfs-prep-non-tc-files/PS-69087-fileshare-methods.playlist | Adds Phase 6 test classes to the curated FileShare-touching playlist (non-browser). |
| docs/temp/tfs-prep-non-tc-files/coverage-progress.md | Introduces a Phase checklist tracker and Phase 6 status/mapping details. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Records/StaticSystem/StaticFileIDTests.cs | Adds an integration test asserting `CreateStaticFileID` writes the DB row and copies the pending file into FMS. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Records/Projects/ProjectCopyFilesTests.cs | Adds an integration test validating file seeding into new project FMS/Photos directories and exercising `CopyPhysicalFiles`. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Records/Portfolios/IgnitionSystemTests.cs | Extends org provisioning tests with coverage/assertions for `GetFileShares` and `CopyCustomReportFiles`. |
</details>
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: The Phase 4 ExternalFileService rules appear to be duplicated: the AmazonS3IntegrationTests/AzureBlobIntegrationTests block already exists earlier (around lines 380–388) and is repeated again here. Please remove the duplicate block to keep the playlist deterministic and easier to maintain.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This doc references a local, user-specific plan path (`C:\Users\acollin\...`) which won’t exist for other contributors/agents. Consider replacing it with a repo-relative path or a link/instructions on where to find the plan artifact so the tracker remains portable.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: CustomTestCleanupAsync deletes the created DMSFiles/FileVersions rows, but StaticSystem caches the static file id per-organization in a private static dictionary. After this test runs, the cache can still point at a now-deleted FileID, which can break later calls to GetStaticFileID/GetOrCreateStaticFileID in the same test process (stale cache). To avoid cross-test flakiness, consider isolating this test in a throwaway organization/project, or avoid deleting the row (or clear the StaticSystem cache for the org as part of cleanup).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The test asserts photo seed files already exist in the new project (lines 69–73), then later calls ProjectSystem.CopyPhysicalFiles (line 87). In ProjectSystem.CopyPhysicalFiles the photo copy uses File.Copy without overwrite (Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ProjectSystem.cs:1066-1070), so re-invoking it when photos already exist will throw/log an error and short-circuit the photo-copy portion. Consider deleting the destination photo files before the direct invocation (similar to the DMS delete) or otherwise isolating the direct-call validation so the test both stays noise-free and actually validates the photo-copy path.
review_comments.author: Copilot

+

+

+
+## Phase 0 — Findings & Open Questions
+
+### Resolved by code/build evidence — Prerequisite #1: `DMSSystem.GetFMSDirectory(int projectID)` overload
+
+**Status:** Technically resolved; disposition still requires a team decision.
+
+**Question (from plan):** Confirm whether the unresolved `DMSSystem.GetFMSDirectory(int projectID)` overload referenced by `SearchSystem.SearchPrologWebFolders` (#45) and `IndexProcess.StartWork` (#64) compiles, is dead code, or comes from an external assembly. **Block Phase 8 until resolved.**
+
+**Answer (from build evidence on this branch):** Both call sites are **dead code that does not compile** in the active build. No external assembly defines a `DMSSystem.GetFMSDirectory(int)` overload. There is therefore nothing to test for #45 / #64 in their current form.
+
+#### Evidence
+
+- The only definition of `DMSSystem` in the repo is `Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/DMSSystem.cs` (`public class DMSSystem : SystemBase` in namespace `ProjectSight.DataAccess`). It contains **no** `GetFMSDirectory` member of any signature; the only `GetFMSDirectory` mention inside `DMSSystem.cs` is the call `new ProjectSightDMSService(portfolioDB.RequestedByObject).GetFMSDirectory()` (instance, no-arg) at line 2658.
+- The two existing `GetFMSDirectory` definitions live on `ProjectSightDMSService`:
+    - `public string GetFMSDirectory()` (instance, no args) — `Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/Services/ProjectSightDMS/ProjectSightDMSService.cs:3480`
+    - `public static string GetFMSDirectory(IDBHelper portfolioDB)` — same file, line 3521
+    - **No `(int projectID)` overload exists on `ProjectSightDMSService` or anywhere else.**
+- A whole-repo search for `GetFMSDirectory` finds zero matches under `Common/` (`GCCM.Common.SystemFramework`) and zero matches anywhere outside the two `*.cs` files listed above.
+- `SearchSystem.cs` is **explicitly excluded from compilation** in the only csproj that contains it:
+    - `Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ProjectSight.BusinessLayer.DataAccess.csproj` line 27 — `<Compile Remove="SearchSystem.cs" />`
+    - No conditional `<Compile Include>` re-adds it elsewhere in the file.
+- `IndexProcess.cs` lives in `Core/Servers/IndexServer/ProjectSight.Servers.IndexServer.csproj`. That csproj is **not referenced from any `*.sln` file** in the repository (`All.sln`, `WebOnly.sln`, `APIOnly.sln`, `ProjectExportServiceOnly.sln`, `ServerMonitorOnly.sln`, `WebUI.sln`, `Office365.sln`, `HPBudgetMapping.sln`, `C1Reporting.sln`, `QATestApp.sln` — none reference `IndexServer`). It also still targets `net4.7` / `LangVersion 5`, consistent with abandoned legacy code.
+- `ITCMigrationReversalSystem.cs` and `Document Search\SearchParameters.cs` are also `<Compile Remove>`'d in `Core/Common/Components/ProjectSight.Common.Components.csproj` (lines 38 and 39), reinforcing that the search/indexing surface is dormant on this branch.
+- The only other consumer of `SearchSystem` is `Core/Web Layer/Unused/DMS/search.aspx.cs` — under the `Unused/` directory.
+
+#### Disposition still needed (lightweight Randy / epic-owner question)
+
+The technical "compiles?" question is answered. What remains is a **policy** call:
+
+> Phase 0 prereq #1 — `SearchSystem.SearchPrologWebFolders` (#45) and `IndexProcess.StartWork` (#64) are confirmed dead code on `wip/adam/tfc-prep-non-tc-files` (and on `lucretia/TrimbleFileService`, which has the same `<Compile Remove>` exclusions): `SearchSystem.cs` is `<Compile Remove>`'d in `ProjectSight.BusinessLayer.DataAccess.csproj`, and `ProjectSight.Servers.IndexServer.csproj` is not referenced from any `.sln`. The `DMSSystem.GetFMSDirectory(int)` overload that both files reference does not exist anywhere in source or in `GCCM.Common.SystemFramework`. **Question for Randy / the search-indexing owner:** Are these methods slated for revival as part of TFS prep, or should Phase 8's #45 / #64 be marked formally deferred in `coverage-progress.md` and removed from the playlist? If revival is planned, when, and which branch?
+
+This question is **non-blocking for Phases 1–7, 9, 10**. It only blocks Phase 8.
+
+### OPEN — Prerequisite #2: `ConnectArchiveSystem.LocatePhysicalLinkedFile` / `LocateFileOrPhoto` scope (#19, #20)
+
+**Status:** Cannot be resolved from code alone. Requires Randy / epic-owner input.
+
+**What code already tells us:**
+
+- Both methods are **private** on `ConnectArchiveSystem` (`Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ConnectArchiveSystem.cs:267, :292`); only entry point is `FileRecoveryJobHandler` (a background job).
+- They run **only in the TC-as-FMS scenario**: read from `FMSDirectory` / `PhotosDirectory` and push to Trimble Connect.
+- Per `PS-69089-read-methods.md` ("ConnectArchiveSystem — Not Obsolete"), the class is actively called by `FileRecoveryJobHandler`; FileShare reads happen specifically in TCAsFMS scenarios.
+- The per-task notes in `test-coverage.md` and `summary-of-methods.md` flag both as TC-recovery (Medium per the task's "TC-exclusion guidance").
+
+**Question to send to Randy / epic owner:**
+
+> PS-69087 Phase 0 prereq #2 — `ConnectArchiveSystem.LocatePhysicalLinkedFile` / `LocateFileOrPhoto` (test-coverage.md #19, #20) are TC-recovery-only paths reached via `FileRecoveryJobHandler`. They read from `FMSDirectory` / `PhotosDirectory` and push files to Trimble Connect. Per the per-task "TC-exclusion guidance" notes the original triage marked them Medium and out of the main coverage scope, but they are real FileShare reads that will become TFS reads after the migration. Should Phase 8 add a `FileRecoveryJobHandler`-driven test for these (in scope), or formally defer them as TC-only and document the deferral in `coverage-progress.md` (out of scope)?
+
+### OPEN — Prerequisite #3: Minimum supported mobile version (PS-69093 follow-up note 6)
+
+**Status:** Cannot be resolved from code alone. Requires Randy / mobile team input.
+
+**What code already tells us:**
+
+- `Core/API Layer/ProjectSight.WebAPI/Controllers/v1/SyncController.cs` source comments document **introduction** versions, not deprecation versions:
+    - `UploadAttachment` (line 2627) — `// Used in Mobile 2.23 and forward`
+    - `UploadFile` (line 2676) — `// Used in Mobile 2.4x and forward`
+    - `UploadPiecemeal` (line 2720) — `// Used in Mobile 2.23 and forward`
+    - `UploadAppend` (line 2821) — `// Used in Mobile 2.4x and forward`
+- The PS-69093 follow-up note 6 references a stale `USED by 2.38 and older mobiles` comment dated 2026-01-01 that no longer matches what is in source. The current comments do not flag any endpoint as legacy.
+- There is no `MinimumSupportedMobileVersion` config or guard in code that we could use to derive the answer.
+
+**Question to send to Randy / the mobile team:**
+
+> PS-69087 Phase 0 prereq #3 (per PS-69093 write-methods follow-up note 6) — `SyncController.UploadAttachment` (test-coverage.md #99 / PS-69093 row 47) and `UploadPiecemeal` (PS-69093 row 49) are tagged in source as "Used in Mobile 2.23 and forward". The newer pair `UploadFile` (#100) / `UploadAppend` (PS-69093 row 50) is tagged "Used in Mobile 2.4x and forward". What is the current minimum supported mobile client version in production? If the floor is at or above the version that introduced `UploadFile` / `UploadAppend`, the legacy `UploadAttachment` + `UploadPiecemeal` pair (and their `InProgressDirectory` chunking) can be removed, eliminating that surface from the TFS migration. If they must stay, Phase 1 (`Mobile/LegacyUploadsRetrievalTests.cs`) should add coverage for both endpoints rather than just the `uploads://` legacy path.
+
+This question is **non-blocking for Phase 1 implementation** — Phase 1 already plans `Mobile/LegacyUploadsRetrievalTests.cs` against `ExternalFileSystem.ConfirmLegacyFileInUploadsDirectoryAsync`. The mobile-version answer changes the *breadth* of Phase 1 / Phase 10, not the start gate. Phase 8 is the only phase explicitly gated.
+

+
+- **`TCMigrationReversalSystem.cs` is also `<Compile Remove>`'d** in `Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ProjectSight.BusinessLayer.DataAccess.csproj` (line 28), and `ITCMigrationReversalSystem.cs` is `<Compile Remove>`'d in `Core/Common/Components/ProjectSight.Common.Components.csproj` (line 38). On this branch, all of `TCMigrationReversalSystem.DownloadFileAsync` / `DownloadAndUpdateDMSFileAsync` / `FetchNonPersistedQueuedFileProperties` (test-coverage.md #90, #91, #54, #53) are uncompiled. **Phase 2 must verify the file is back in the build on whichever branch Phase 2 targets** before its tests can run; the canonical compile-include is presumably restored on `lucretia/TrimbleFileService` when the migration code lands. Phase 2 agent: confirm build on target branch first.
+- The `<Compile Remove>` block also lists `MaterialSystem.cs`, `FACSystem.cs`, and `ERPSystemAvro.cs`, none of which are in the PS-69087 method list — included here for completeness only.
